privileged_info_summary: The data-processor Maven project contains three correctly identified dependency management issues: (1) junit 4.12 is declared with 'compile' scope but used only in test code and should have 'test' scope; (2) joda-time is declared twice with versions 2.9.2 and 2.10.13, requiring removal of the duplicate and retention of the higher version 2.10.13, which is actively used in production code; (3) commons-codec 1.15 is declared in pom.xml but never imported or used in any source file (TextRecord.java, RecordProcessor.java, or RecordProcessorTest.java) and should be removed entirely. All dependency versions are real releases available in Maven Central Repository. The project is synthetic (created for this task) but accurately demonstrates real Maven dependency anti-patterns found in production systems.
